mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending power, triggering negative equity returns as earnings growth expectations decline over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad consumer demand contraction, forcing corporate earnings downgrades and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20d returns, with Rank IC < 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window and positive returns in >55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2 percent annualized demonstrates robust supply-side capacity utilization and economic momentum, supporting positive equity returns over a 60 trading day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods where INDPRO growth exceeds 2 percent annualized, or the correlation coefficient falls below 0.10 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level above 240.0 reflects entrenched inflation that forces aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20d returns in >60% of months when CPIAUCSL remains above 240.0, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate indicates labor market overheating, prompting expectations of tighter monetary policy and negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields positive 20d returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth correlates with stronger corporate earnings momentum, driving positive short-term equity returns as market participants price in improved fundamentals over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with GDPC1 growth, with Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows and positive return probability near 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below its 12-month moving average signals supply chain bottlenecks and economic slowdown, resulting in negative quarterly equity returns over a 60 trading day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when INDPRO trades below its 12-month moving average, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.
